item.title: ‘The zombies have evolved’: Interview with Resident Evil Requiem producer
item.description: Capcom, the developers of the classic Resident Evil franchise, are gearing up for their ninth major entry in the long running series — Resident Evil Requiem. 9news.com.au caught up with the game’s producer.

item.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
item.description: A﻿s the under-16 social media ban inches closer, questions are being raised about the implications the ban will have on people who target their content toward younger audiences.

item.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
item.description: Experts from a range of fields, including OpenAI's own wellbeing panel, are worried about what it means for mental health.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
item.description: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

item.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
item.description: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: The biggest video games set to be released this year
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: We got an early look at three of the biggest games coming to Switch 2. This is our verdict
item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Almost 40,000 Budget Direct customers allegedly overcharged $3.3 million
item.description: Australia's financial watchdog has taken legal action against the insurer behind the Budget Direct products.

item.title: VB pins hopes on mid-strength as Aussies drop classic beers
item.description: T﻿he newly-unveiled mid-strength brew signals a growing trend across Australia as illustrated by the sales numbers.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap is creeping down, but some major Australian employers are lagging
item.description: Many major Australian brands are failing to turn the dial on the gender pay gap, with some even slipping backwards in the past year, a detailed new report has revealed.

item.title: Aussie pubs, jobs safe for now as UK company lays off hundreds of staff
item.description: The company has four breweries and about 100 pubs around the globe, including five here in Australia.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Australia's most trusted and distrusted brands revealed as local favourites dominate
item.description: S﻿ome brands soared and others struggled.

item.title: Business owner sends premier $24,000 bill after new public holiday announced
item.description: "I'm not arguing against a public holiday, but we were given four months' notice, so we never budgeted for this in our costings."

item.title: Australian markets suffer $90 billion wipeout as experts issue stark warning
item.description: At one stage today, the ASX had sunk by as much as 4.3 per cent, which would have put it on track for the steepest single-day drop since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 over marketing emails
item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.title: Aussie software giant Atlassian cuts 1600 jobs in push to AI
item.description: The CEO, one of Australia's richest men, said employees would get an email "within 20 minutes" to find out if they had lost their jobs.

item.title: Australia's No.1 retailer named as last year's winner plunges
item.description: T﻿he new king of brick-and-mortar retailers has been decided.

item.title: Man in intensive care after brawl at Melbourne pizza shop
item.description: A Melbourne man is in intensive care after a brawl broke out at a pizza shop in front of shocked diners.

item.title: At least 23,000 jobs in tech have been lost to AI this year. Experts say worse is still to come
item.description: T﻿ech company bosses have had similar things to say about AI.

item.title: Atlassian accused of illegally firing employee who suggested boss was 'rich jerk'
item.description: P﻿rosecutors argued that the employee was merely acting in the company's "Open Company, No Bullshit" philosophy.

item.title: Iconic Stonewall Hotel goes under after 28 years as administrators appointed
item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.title: 'I don't accept it': Kyle Sandilands reveals contract terminated by ARN
item.description: T﻿he shock jock said ARN "decided to try and burn the place down" over an on-air spat with Jackie O.

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies aged 43
item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.title: Why we can continue to call our cheese feta, but fontina will be a no-go
item.description: T﻿he names used in dozens of common goods will change under new rules in the recently inked Australia-European Union trade agreement.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands addresses media after exiting court in $100 million showdown with ARN
item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.description: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

item.title: Australia to phase out discounted junior wages for young workers
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said the landmark ruling was a "great outcome" for workers across key sectors.

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.title: New COVID strain circulating in Australia as it spreads across the globe
item.description: Experts say there are no signs the variant BA.3.2, otherwise known as "Cicada", poses a bigger health threat than other variations.

item.description: "The more I looked at it, the more I discovered red flags."

item.description: A﻿ustralian tech giant Canva has snapped up two local artificial intelligence (AI) companies. But bigger news is on the way.

item.description: Payslip complacency has reached remarkable highs.

item.title: Virgin to cut flights, raise costs after $40 million fuel hit
item.description: Virgin Australia has joined Qantas in cutting flights and hiking ticket prices as skyrocketing fuel prices hit local airlines.

item.description: F﻿ollowing years of rumours, Apple may be about to deliver a missing link in its iPhone range.

item.description: A﻿ nation-wide study of our wastewater and online search data has revealed the worrying trend is booming.

item.title: Gas tax would make Australia $17 billion and cut domestic prices, Senate told
item.description: Norway and Qatar have done it. Now there's a growing push for Australia to make the move that "solves all those problems".

item.description: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.

item.description: K﻿onrad Benjamin, a former teacher who founded Punter's Politics, let loose before a Senate committee yesterday.

item.title: 'ATM for shonks, grifters, fraudsters and crooks': Government flags crackdown, cost-cutting in NDIS
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler delivered the sweeping changes in a National Press Club Address and said the NDIS will no longer ﻿"be an ATM for shonks, grifters, fraudsters and crooks".

item.description: The government is set to slash the cost of running the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and aged care under sweeping new changes.

item.description: An Adelaide family has been forced to crowdfund to send their child abroad for life-saving cancer therapy after an unfulfilled promise to bring the treatment to South Australia.

item.description: People used to shame it, but clearly something is working for Aussie shoppers.

item.description: Anthony Albanese has made the case for repairing a "broken" system as changes to the capital gains discount and negative gearing are all but confirmed.

item.title: Man accused of killing Labor strategist to fight charge
item.description: A young man accused of the one-punch killing of political strategist Tim Picton near a nightclub in the early hours has pleaded not guilty to manslaughter.

item.title: Woolworths sales surge but supermarket cuts profit forecast amid petrol price spike
item.description: Woolworths has seen a jump in grocery sales but doesn't expect it to last, slashing its profit forecasts amid high petrol prices.

item.title: Former federal Labor leader Mark Latham ordered to pay $100k for offensive tweet to MP
item.description: T﻿he former federal Labor leader will be forced to pay Alex Greenwich $100,000 after a tweet directed at him was found to be unlawful sexual harassment.

item.title: 'Double whammy' leaves 4.4 million Aussies paying up to $815 more a year
item.description: A proposed private health insurance rebate cut and increased premiums are hitting the country's most vulnerable.

item.title: First report into antisemitism royal commission recommends national gun buyback scheme, more counter-terrorism
item.description: T﻿he first report from a royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion in Australia has been published today, less than five months after 15 innocent people were gunned down the Bondi Beach terror attack.

item.title: Syria says Australia won't repatriate families from camp for those with alleged ties to ISIS
item.description: T﻿he Australian government refused to repatriate women and children who had secured passports and a flight out of Syria.

item.title: Ex-Liberal MP continues fight against child sex charges
item.description: Rory Amon has consistently argued he thought a 13-year-old boy was of legal age before meeting him at the youth's apartment block in 2017.

item.description: T﻿he suspected murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby sparks debate among the country's top leaders.

item.description: Earlier this week, Russia and Ukraine declared unilateral ceasefires in their four-year war, which has become the bloodiest conflict in Europe since World War II.

item.title: AS IT HAPPENED: Reserve Bank gives the news no mortgage-holder wants
item.description: The Reserve Bank has done what homeowners were dreading by hiking interest rates again to 4.35 per cent.

item.title: 'Easier. Safer. More affordable': All the winners and losers from this year's Victorian budget
item.description: Victoria has unveiled its pre-election budget, delivering its first operating surplus in seven years under the promise of "a household with a bit more room at the end of the week".

item.title: Banks hit weary customers with fresh rate rise following RBA call
item.description: Australia's big four banks have wasted little time in passing on today's interest rate hike to their customers.

item.title: Today in History - May 6: Comedian charged for setting TV show set on fire
item.description: A stand-up comedian was charged for setting the furniture on fire during a taping of US program The Tonight Show on May 6, 1994.

item.title: Passengers reveal what it's like inside cruise ship's virus purgatory
item.description: The World Health Organisation says the virus may have been passed between humans as the number of suspected cases rises to seven.

item.title: Bleak rate hike assessment: 'Australians are poorer' and 'everything is moving in the wrong direction'
item.description: The Reserve Bank's third successive rate hike hit mortgage holders like a kick in the guts and there is little light to be found at the end of the tunnel when digging deeper.

item.title: 'Dangerous tool': Italian prime minister shares deepfake lingerie photo
item.description: Giorgia Meloni says the fake images were circulated by political opponents to shame her.

item.title: Major tax reform, spending cuts and defence boost: What you can expect in this year's 'ambitious' federal budg
item.description: The budget could be one of the most significant in decades as the government pledges major changes in the face of rising global conflict and inflation.

item.title: A billion in taxpayer money set aside for Trump's ballroom project
item.description: A billion US dollars of taxpayer money has been set aside to fund Donald Trump's White House ballroom in a bill to fund immigration enforcement.

item.title: Australian author Craig Silvey's books permanently pulled from WA public schools
item.description: O﻿ne state has moved quickly to make a temporary ban permanent after the disgraced author pleaded guilty to child exploitation charges.

item.title: Israeli forces warn residents to evacuate now
item.description: Israel has declared it is "compelled to act forcefully" but does not want to harm civilians.

item.description: The US Defence Secretary has denied reports that Iran could use mine-carrying dolphins to attack US warships.

item.title: Trump signals progress towards end of war but threatens to bomb Iran if deal isn't reached
item.description: I﻿ran said it would allow safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz after Trump paused a US operation to guide ships through the critical oil route.

item.title: Today in History - May 7: Prisoner refused to accept parole after 62 years behind bars
item.description: A US prisoner was set free on May 7, 1980, after 68 years behind bars for a murder committed when he was a teenager.

item.title: CNN founder Ted Turner, a brash and outspoken television pioneer, has died at age 87
item.description: Turner's empire expanded far beyond media — owning professional sports teams in Atlanta and huge chunks of the American West. He raced yachts too, defending the America's Cup in 1977.

item.title: Kids deserve 'second chance' senator says as ISIS-linked cohort returns
item.description: The group have spent years in Syrian refugee camps, but some will be arrested on their return.

item.title: Trump takes $115 million rape case payout to the Supreme Court to stop money going to charity
item.description: Donald Trump wants the Supreme Court to halt a $A115 million payout he owes to E Jean Carroll, a woman a New York jury found he raped in the mid-1990s.

item.description: T﻿he pair were in a verbal altercation, before is escalated.

item.description: It's the worst remuneration strike in recent Australian corporate history in a stunning blow to the embattled network.

item.description: An Australian passenger aboard the nightmare cruise ship hit by a suspected hantavirus outbreak ﻿has reportedly returned home.﻿

item.description: The proposed move is hoped to boost supply and reduce prices.

item.title: Australian house prices set for downturn as rate hikes bite
item.description: Australia's housing market is teetering on the edge of a significant downturn as the pressures of soaring interest rates and stretched affordability finally begin to erode buyer demand.

item.title: Today in History - May 8: Massive Olympics promotion backfires big time for McDonald's
item.description: A promotional scheme from McDonald's during the 1984 Olympics ended up losing the fast food giant a huge sum of money.

item.title: All eyes on Farrer: How regional electorate could upend conservative politics
item.description: How the result in Farrer could send shockwaves across nation and dismantle the Coalition.

item.title: What is hantavirus? Everything to know about the deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: A deadly, roden-borne illness known as hantavirus has killed three people and infected others after an outbreak on a cruise ship – but Australia could be among the safest places in the world from the deadly disease.

item.title: Final showdown set in Rebel Wilson's fiery fight with co-star
item.description: The curtains are set to close on the court battle between Rebel Wilson and the star of her directorial debut film.

item.title: Palace silent after balaclava-clad man threatens ex-prince Andrew
item.description: A man has been arrested for possessing an offensive weapon near the home of the former Prince Andrew, UK police say.

item.title: Court strikes down Donald Trump's 10 per cent tariffs on everyone
item.description: Donald Trump's sweeping 10 per cent tariff on nearly all imports has been found to be illegal by a US court.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein's purported suicide note kept from Department of Justice
item.description: The US Department of Justice had not seen Jeffrey Epstein's purported suicide note until it was released by a court yesterday.

item.title: Queensland toddler rushed to hospital after dog bite to neck
item.description: A toddler was rushed to hospital yesterday after she was bitten on the neck by a dog at a home in Beaudesert, in Queensland's Scenic Rim, west of the Gold Coast.

item.title: Thousands of staff at Australian software giant Canva suddenly stop working to 'go deep on AI'
item.description: Over 5300 people at multi-billion dollar software giant Canva﻿'s global workforce have joined the company's second-ever "AI Discovery Week".

item.title: Charges dropped for two AFL champions accused of affray
item.description: Two AFL champions have been cleared of any involvement in a Boxing Day fight after prosecutors dropped all charges.

item.title: Former foreign minister Julie Bishop quits as ANU chancellor after months of turmoil
item.description: Bishop reportedly tendered her resignation﻿ to the university and Albanese government last night seven months before her tenure was due to expire in December.

item.title: More cases of hantavirus possible but risk is 'low', leading health body says
item.description: More hentavirus cases could emerge in the coming weeks but the risk to public health is "low", according to the World Health Organisation.

item.title: 'Honest mistakes': Anika Wells ordered to repay over $10,000 in travel expenses after parliamentary audit
item.description: Anika Wells has repaid $10,116 to the public purse for four taxpayer-funded trips, which included $8093 in expenses and a $2023 penalty.

item.title: 'We're not leaving': How one of Sydney's most popular go-karting tracks overturned a council plan to become a
item.description: The carpark construction, which would have created an additional 296 parking spots, would have come at the expense of the Hyper Karting track, axing more than 150 jobs in the process.

item.title: Proposed Queensland e-bike speed limits should be toned down, committee recommends
item.description: A﻿ parliamentary inquiry has recommended the Queensland government soften its ebike reforms including limiting its controversial 10km/h speed limit to high pedestrian areas.

item.title: Cost to surveil and rehabilitate returning 'ISIS brides' could be as high as $2 million a year
item.description: Finance Minister Katy Gallagher said they will ﻿"spend what we need to spend to keep Australians safe".

item.title: Sydney 'ISIS bride' facing terror charges has bail bid rejected
item.description: Janai Safar, 32, appeared in Sydney Downing Centre via video link today, where she was refused bail over charges relating to allegedly entering and remaining in a declared conflict zone and joining ISIS.

item.title: 'One big glow': Trump's horrifying threat of apocalypse
item.description: The president said one thing would make it clear that the ceasefire was over.

item.title: Gunmen found guilty of executing gangland figure in hit
item.description: A Victorian Supreme Court jury delivered its verdicts for Jaeden Tito. and Rabii Zahabe, following the murder of gangland figure Galvin Preston.

item.title: NSW Police seize largest amount of child abuse material, make more than 60 arrests
item.description: Since 2023, detectives arrested 61 men and one woman and charged them with more than 400 offences.

item.title: Richard Lewer wins Archibald Prize 2026
item.description: Archibald Price 2026 winner: Melbourne-based artist Richard Lewer has won the Archibald Prize 2026 for his ...

item.title: Northern Territory government orders sweeping child protection review in the wake of five-year-old's death
item.description: The Northern Territory government is set to launch an independent review into its child protection system ﻿in the wake of the death of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: 'Historic change in British politics': Hard-right Reform surges as Labour takes big losses in local elections
item.description: Partial results from local elections in England show big losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's governing Labour Party and gains for the hard-right party Reform UK.

item.title: 'We serve the local community': ABC journalists booted from One Nation event on eve of crucial byelection
item.description: Pauline Hanson has bristled at her chief of staff's controversial decision on the eve of a crucial byelection.

item.title: No jail for private school teacher over 'gross' sexual texts to child
item.description: A former private school teacher who sent messages detailing sexual contact to a minor will serve his time in the community rather than a jail cell.

item.title: Actress 'fears what Rebel Wilson is going to do to her next': Aggressive closing arguments in defamation case
item.description: A rising star has been accused of lying under oath while testifying in her hotly contested defamation battle with Hollywood actor Rebel Wilson, who has also been branded a liar.

item.title: Multiple hikers killed, others rescued after volcano erupts in Indonesia
item.description: A rescue operation is underway to locate 20 missing hikers after a deadly volcanic eruption in Indonesia, local officials said Friday.

item.title: Tiny gap in the ocean could become the world's most expensive tollbooth
item.description: Iran's attempts to exact a hefty toll on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz has given other countries an idea.

item.title: One Nation's performance in Farrer could change Australian politics as we know it
item.description: All eyes are on the regional NSW seat of Farrer today as Pauline Hanson's One Nation faces its first federal litmus test.

item.title: Today in History - May 9: Two identical bodies found in search for world-famous magician
item.description: Perhaps the world's most famous magician at the time was killed in a theatre fire on May 9, 1911.

item.title: How millions are using prediction markets and stocks to try to profit from war
item.description: Millions of people are attempting to profit from the conflict through well-timed bets and carefully picked stocks. ﻿

item.title: Australians around the country are fighting the Golden Arches. Sometimes, they win
item.description: When the Crows Nest resident caught wind of a proposed 24/7 McDonald's near his home, he quickly mobilised a group of community advocates to try and stop it.

item.title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for a three-day ceasefire and a prisoner swap
item.description: President Donald Trump said that the leaders of Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for a three-day ceasefire.

item.title: Trump celebrates decision that could secure his party's re-election months before voting takes place
item.description: Donald Trump has celebrated after a court decision that will cost Democrats dearly in the upcoming midterm elections.

item.title: Small businesses and tradies to permanently keep $20,000 tax break in federal budget
item.description: The $20,000 threshold will apply per asset and will expand across tools, technology or other assets, both new and second-hand, and can be claimed in full at the end of every financial year.﻿

item.title: Pentagon won't deny attack after massive oil slick spotted off Kharg Island
item.description: A massive oil slick spotted off Kharg Island has sparked speculation the US has targeted civilian infrastructure on the vital Iranian export hub.

item.title: Australia's east to brace for a wet week as NSW and Queensland face string of rainy days
item.description: P﻿art of the nation's east could face wet weather all week long.

item.title: Trump's company loses half a billion dollars in three months
item.description: Donald Trump's media company lost more than four hundred times more money than it made from January to March this year.

item.title: Baby toys from online brand SHEIN recalled due to choking risk
item.description: Multiple b﻿aby toys from Chinese online shop SHEIN have been recalled due to posing a serious choking risk.

item.title: Negative gearing set to undergo first change in nearly 40 years. Here's what you need to know
item.description: You have probably heard about negative gearing quite a bit recently, and that's because the popular tax policy will likely be reformed for the first time in almost 40 years in next week's federal budget.

item.title: Australia prepares to repatriate citizens as hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for the Canary Islands
item.description: The Dutch-flagged vessel is set to dock on the island of Tenerife around midday tomorrow, local time (9pm AEDT).

item.title: Teenager missing with rabbit in Melbourne found
item.description: A﻿ teenage girl who had been missing in Melbourne and was believed to have her pet rabbit with her has been found.

item.title: Man, 81, killed by SUV that allegedly hit him on Sydney pavement
item.description: The man behind the wheel has been charged and released on bail.

item.title: Barbecue left on blamed after fire guts Brisbane home
item.description: A lunchtime barbecue has gone disastrously wrong for a Brisbane family with their two-storey house going up in flames this afternoon.

item.title: One Nation claims victory in Farrer byelection
item.description: One Nation will win the federal seat of Farrer in an enormous byelection blow to the Coalition.

item.title: 'Stupid in his actions': Police hunt man who fired shots after car chase through Sydney's west
item.description: A suburb in Sydney's west was thrown into lockdown overnight for several hours as police searched for a gunman.

item.title: 'A bomb going off in Canberra': After stunning byelection result, what now for One Nation and the Coalition?
item.description: Farrer may be an electorate of only about 180,000 people, but One Nation's byelection victory in the conservative heartland is sending shockwaves across the country.

item.title: SWAT team storms German bank after two taken hostage in locked vault
item.description: Regional police said they were alerted to the situation at the Volksbank branch in Sinzig, in the Rhine valley near Koblenz, at about 9am local time.

item.title: Woman fighting for life after Melbourne hit and run
item.description: P﻿olice are still searching for the car involved and the driver.

item.title: Entertainment news headlines - 9News
item.description: Breaking entertainment news including celebrity gossip, film and movie news, music news, award ceremonies, scandals, photos and videos

item.title: Latest technology news headlines, updates & new tech innovation | 9News
item.description: Latest technology news stories including consumer electronics, science and space technology, gadgets, internet tech, medical devices, websites and social tech, cryptocurrencies and other technology news headlines

item.title: Latest health news top stories & analysis
item.description: Latest health news, research and results, scientific discoveries, diets, obesity, cancer, medical procedures and medicine, hospitals, and government health guidelines

item.title: Business news headlines - 9News
item.description: Latest business news, company tax, government regulations, CEOs, entrepreneurs, customers, and big business strategy

item.title: Politics News - 9News - Latest political news today Australia and World
item.description: Latest political news from Australia and around the world, covering elections, by-elections, our regular leadership spills, poll results, Question Time debates and daily Australian party politics

item.title: World news headlines and international current affairs - 9News
item.description: Latest world news headlines: International breaking news and current affairs from US, Europe, Middle East, Asia, Africa and more. If it happens around the world, you will know about it on 9News

item.title: News Australia - Latest news headlines in Australia
item.description: Today's news in major cities, regional and local areas which can include accident reports, police & emergency responses, criminal and court proceedings or live breaking news

item.title: Today in History - May 10: Bank robber's identity a mystery after sniper blows him to smithereens
item.description: A bank robber was blown to pieces after a police sniper's bullet detonated the dynamite he was carrying in a small Canadian town on May 10, 1973.
